=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (12:14-12:27) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn("arrowWithPipe")(function*() { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"

[D2] (13:3-15:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D3] (17:14-17:40) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithPipe")(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations)`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"

[D4] (18:10-20:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D5] (23:17-23:44) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithPipe")(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations)`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"

[D6] (24:10-26:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fn("arrowWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
});

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"));

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D1] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"));

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () => Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1));

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D3] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"));

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D3] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"));

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}


=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (with span from withSpan)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithPipe")(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged);


=== [D5] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fnUntraced(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"));


=== [D5] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export const functionDeclarationWithPipe = Effect.fn(function* () {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42);
}, Effect.map(x => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"));


=== [D6] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
// because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
// the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
// Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
//
// Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
// the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.

export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))

export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
  }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
}

export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
  return Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignoreLogged, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
}
